title: Daemon Workflow Evidence
assertions: workflows with executable checks must either publish evidence artifacts or be registered as accepted gaps
artifact uploads must fail closed with if-no-files-found:error
each evidence-out path must be uploaded by a strict artifact step
every evidence-out capable tool must be invoked by at least one workflow
every evidence-out capable tool must have an evidence-out invocation bound to an uploaded artifact
workflow inventory must include both .yml and .yaml GitHub Actions files
accepted gaps are debt, not completion
daemon executable workflow evidence must converge to zero accepted gaps

loop.observation: Daemon has many public GitHub Actions workflows, but command-only checks can pass or fail without a durable JSON evidence artifact, making loop-engineering claims weaker than control-plane workflow evidence.
loop.hypothesis: A daemon workflow evidence manifest can classify every executable workflow, fail on non-strict uploads or missing evidence-out bindings, and keep accepted gaps at zero once legacy command-only workflows are bound to evidence tools.
loop.execute: Scan .yml and .yaml GitHub Actions workflows, classify evidence coverage, require fail-closed artifact uploads, require every evidence-out path to be uploaded, compare evidence-out capable tools with workflow invocations, and render a generated workflow evidence reader.
loop.evaluate: The verifier fails on unregistered executable workflows, non-strict artifact uploads, missing evidence uploads, evidence-capable tools without workflow coverage, stale generated docs, unused accepted gaps, or workflow extension drift.
loop.retrospective: Daemon workflow observability is now an executable migration surface: covered evidence workflows stay green, broad CI emits compact evidence, and future workflow drift cannot remain silent.
